description_long: This is a medium-sized bird with a robust body, covered in mostly dark gray to black feathers with subtle brownish undertones. The head is rounded and the beak is strong, slightly curved, and pale in color, typical of corvids. The wings are folded tightly against the body, and the tail is short and appears slightly rounded. The legs and feet are visible, appearing pale and somewhat desiccated, suggesting the bird may be a preserved specimen. The plumage is smooth and uniform, with no prominent markings or color patches. The overall appearance is that of a common urban bird, likely a member of the crow or raven family.
description: A dark gray to black bird with a robust body, pale curved beak, and smooth, uniform plumage. The legs are pale and the tail is short and rounded.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bird, with the head, beak, body, and extended legs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded head, pale beak, and symmetry of the body and wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the bird's profile, smooth plumage, and the short, rounded tail.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail's rounded shape and the pale, extended legs.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid dark gray to black with subtle brownish gradients
